channel.title: AI Viral News
channel.description: Curated viral AI news from Reddit + blogs — updated every 4 hours

item.title: [📰] Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
item.description:
Nous Research, the open-source artificial intelligence startup backed by crypto venture firm Paradigm, released a new competitive programming model on Monday that it says matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems — trained in just four days using 48 of Nvidia's latest B200 graphics processors.

The model, called

item.title: [📰] Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
item.description:
Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users — and according to company insiders, the team built the entire feature in approximately a week and a half, largely using Claude Code itself.

The launch marks a major inflection point i...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
item.description:
Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot, the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification tool into what executives describe as a fully powered AI agent capable of searching enterprise data, drafting documents, and taking action on behalf of employees.

The new Slackbot, now generally available to

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
item.description:
Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs, needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $100 million offers seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a billboard in San Francisco displayi...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
item.description:
The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive.

Claude Code, Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, debug, and deploy code autonomously, has captured the imagination of software developers worldwide. But its pricing — ranging from $20 to $200 per month depending on usage — has sparked a growing rebellion among the very programmers it aims...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description:
Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the limitations of legacy cloud infrastructure.

TQ Ventures led the round, with participation from FPV Ventures...

📡 VentureBeat AI · 📍 VentureBeat AI

item.title: [📰] Import AI 450: China’s electronic warfare model; traumatized LLMs; and a scaling law for cyberattacks
[📰] Import AI 451: Political superintelligence; Google’s society of minds, and a robot drummer
[📰] Import AI 452: Scaling laws for cyberwar; rising tides of AI automation; and a puzzle over gDP forecasting
[📰] Import AI 453: Breaking AI agents; MirrorCode; and ten views on gradual disempowerment
[📰] Import AI 454: Automating alignment research; safety study of a Chinese model; HiFloat4
[📰] Import AI 455: Automating AI Research
[📰] Import AI 456: RSI and economic growth; radical optionality for AI regulation; and a neural computer
[📰] Import AI 457: AI stuxnet; cursed Muon optimizer; and positive alignment
[📰] Scaling creativity in the age of AI
item.description:
Storytelling is core to humanity’s DNA, stemming from our impulse to express ideals, warnings, hopes, and experiences. Technology has always been woven through the medium and the distribution: from early humans’ innovation of natural pigments and charcoals for cave paintings to literal representation by the camera. The landscape of storytelling continues to shift under our…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Roundtables: Can AI Learn to Understand the World?
item.description:
Listen to the session or watch below AI companies want to build systems that understand the external world and overcome the limitations of LLMs. Recent developments have brought world models to the forefront of the AI discussion. Watch a conversation with editor in chief Mat Honan, senior AI editor Will Douglas Heaven, and AI reporter…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Google I/O showed how the path for AI-driven science is shifting
item.description:
During Tuesday’s Google I/O keynote, Demis Hassabis, the CEO of Google DeepMind, proclaimed that we are currently “standing in the foothills of the singularity.” It was a striking statement—the singularity is the theoretical future moment when AI rapidly exceeds human intelligence and dramatically transforms the world. But what struck me as I listened in the…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Specialization Beats Scale: A Strategic Variable Most AI Procurement Decisions Overlook
item.description: 📡 HuggingFace Blog · 📍 HuggingFace Blog

item.title: [📰] Harness, Scaffold, and the AI Agent Terms Worth Getting Right
item.description: 📡 HuggingFace Blog · 📍 HuggingFace Blog

item.title: [📰] It’s time to address the looming crisis in entry-level work
item.description:
Artificial intelligence has not so far produced a clean story of mass unemployment. Aggregate employment in developed countries remains broadly stable, and recent assessments have found limited evidence that AI has shifted the headline numbers. But a troubling change may be hiding beneath the surface: the quiet weakening of the first rung of the career…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] A reality check on the AI jobs hysteria
item.description:
Haven’t you heard? White-collar jobs are going away, decimated by AI. Waves of layoffs in the tech sector (most recently at Coinbase and Meta and Cisco) are said to presage what will soon come for all of us knowledge workers. But before you quit your job as a software developer or financial analyst—or tech journalist—and…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Import AI 458: Reckoning with the future; and a singularity story
[📰] Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
item.description:
Amid rapidly growing adoption of enterprise-level AI agents, there’s a disconnect emerging between ambition and execution.  Although 85% of organizations say they want to be agentic within the next three years, 76% say their current operations and infrastructure can’t support that change. They cite a lack of readiness across people, processes, and workflows.  The sticky…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] The AI Hype Index: AI gets booed in graduation season
item.description:
It is one thing to say AI will change the world. It is another to expect the class of 2026 to applaud it. In fact, when former Google CEO Eric Schmidt told University of Arizona graduates that their task is to help shape AI, he was met with a resounding chorus of boos. “I can…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
item.description:
Pope Leo XIV’s new encyclical on artificial intelligence includes a statement that warrants serious attention from technologists and policymakers: “Technology is never neutral.” Magnifica Humanitas (“Magnificent Humanity”) is a clarion call to all people to act with courage and solidarity as we enter an age already being transformed by artificial intelligence, the greatest change in…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Trajectory Releases a Concurrent Multi-LoRA Training Stack for Continual Learning, Reporting a 2.81× Experiment-Throughput Gain
item.description:
Trajectory, working with UC Berkeley Sky Lab and Anyscale, built a concurrent multi-LoRA training stack for continual learning. It maps each RL experiment to a dedicated LoRA adapter on an always-hot engine, reporting a 2.81× end-to-end experiment-throughput gain over a single-tenant baseline with no reward regression. The code is open-sourced in NovaSky-AI/SkyRL.

The post

item.title: [📰] An Implementation of the Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit for Safe AI Agent Tool Use with Policies, Approvals, Audit Logs, and Risk Controls
item.description:
In this tutorial, we build a governed AI-agent workflow using Microsoft’s Agent Governance Toolkit as the reference point. We create a Colab-ready implementation where agents do not directly execute tools; instead, every action first passes through a governance layer that checks the agent’s identity, trust score, risk tier, requested tool, action type, sensitivity level, and […]

The post

item.title: [📰] Welcome NVIDIA Cosmos 3: The First Open Omni-model for Physical AI Reasoning and Action
item.description: 📡 HuggingFace Blog · 📍 HuggingFace Blog

item.title: [📰] Import AI 459: AI oversight is difficult; scaling laws for protein folding models; and pricing the extinction risk of AI systems
[📰] Beyond LLMs: Why Scalable Enterprise AI Adoption Depends on Agent Logic
item.description: 📡 HuggingFace Blog · 📍 HuggingFace Blog

item.title: [📰] How to Speed Up Transformer Training Using NVIDIA Apex (FusedAdam, FusedLayerNorm) and Native torch.amp
item.description:
We build NVIDIA Apex from source, detect fused kernels, and benchmark FusedAdam, FusedLayerNorm, and torch.amp in Transformer training.

The post How to Speed Up Transformer Training Using NVIDIA Apex (FusedAdam, FusedLayerNorm) and Native torch.amp appeared first on MarkTechPost.

📡 MarkTechPost · 📍 MarkTechPost

item.title: [📰] JetBrains Releases Mellum2: A 12B MoE Model for Fast, Specialized Tasks in Multi-Model AI Pipelines
item.description:
JetBrains releases Mellum2 under Apache 2.0 — a 12B MoE model trained on 10.6 trillion tokens for AI workflows.

The post JetBrains Releases Mellum2: A 12B MoE Model for Fast, Specialized Tasks in Multi-Model AI Pipelines appeared first on MarkTechPost.

📡 MarkTechPost · 📍 MarkTechPost

item.title: [📰] How small businesses can leverage AI
item.description:
This article is from Making AI Work, MIT Technology Review’s limited-run newsletter examining how to apply LLMs across industries. To receive it in your inbox,sign up here. From accounting to design to market research and product development, there’s a staggering breadth of skills needed to run a business. A large company can hire experts to…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Adafruit receives demand letter from Fenwick legal counsel on behalf of Flux.ai
item.description:
Adafruit receives demand letter from Fenwick legal counsel on behalf of Flux.ai

👍 571 · 📍 Hacker News

item.title: [📰] Rehumanizing global health care with agentic AI
item.description:
The global health care sector is under increasing strain.  Decades of chronic underinvestment and constraints in recruitment have coincided with a surge in demand for services for aging populations. Gaps in provision are already taking a toll, with fragmented access to care and high rates of stress and burnout among staff. And it’s getting worse.…

📡 MIT Tech Review AI · 📍 MIT Tech Review AI

item.title: [📰] Anthropic scales Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
item.description:
Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing, its security vulnerability program, and access to Mythos to 150 organizations across 15 countries — targeting critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications where a cyberattack could affect 100 million people.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
item.description:
OpenAI released a set of six plug-ins aimed at specific jobs: data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Available from within the Codex app, each of the new tools bundles integrations, instructions, and context to allow Codex to approximate a specific job.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] How we index images for RAG
item.description:
How we index images for RAG

👍 51 · 📍 Hacker News

item.title: [📰] Trump signs narrower executive order on AI oversight after industry objections
item.description:
After industry objections, President Trump signed a revised AI executive order requiring only voluntary prerelease government reviews of advanced models.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Trump signs downsized AI order after weeks of reversals
item.description:
Trump signs downsized AI order after weeks of reversals

👍 140 · 📍 Hacker News

GitHub Copilot App

item.title: [📰] Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
item.description:
The specification lets developer, compliance, and security teams define their own policies for agents to follow in portable policy files.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Google rolls out fake call detection to protect against AI deepfake impersonation scams
item.description:
As people increasingly refuse to answer calls from unknown numbers, scammers are shifting their tactics by spoofing trusted phone numbers and using AI deepfake technology to sound like authority figures, family members, or employers.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Android phones will soon be able to detect spoofed calls and impersonation scams
item.description:
Google's June Android feature drop includes more scam detection, more AirDrop, and yes, more AI.

📡 Ars Technica AI · 📍 Ars Technica AI

item.title: [📰] Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant
item.description:
Launched at Build, Microsoft Scout is a new AI assistant meant to bring the power and flexibility of OpenClaw into the Microsoft 365 system.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Martin Scorsese becomes the latest — and most unlikely — Hollywood voice for AI
item.description:
The caveat is that one of the world's most famous living directors is using the tech solely for storyboarding.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Mathematicians warn of AI threats to profession as industry encroaches
item.description:
International Mathematical Union endorses warning about tech industry influence.

📡 Ars Technica AI · 📍 Ars Technica AI

item.title: [📰] Microsoft announces Scout, an autonomous AI agent built on OpenClaw
item.description:
Microsoft announces Scout, an autonomous AI agent built on OpenClaw

👍 71 · 📍 Hacker News

item.title: [📰] New Microsoft tool lets devs spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions
item.description:
Microsoft on Tuesday took the wraps off Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing, an open source framework for spinning up AI evaluations.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
item.description:
Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.

📡 TechCrunch AI · 📍 TechCrunch AI

item.title: [📰] Amazon-owned Ring should pay Americans for scanning their faces, lawsuit says
item.description:
Lawsuit: Ring cameras scan guests and passersby and use AI to identify faces.

📡 Ars Technica AI · 📍 Ars Technica AI
